feed.title: SAP Community - SAP AI Services
feed.subtitle: SAP AI Services Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Generative AI Powered IAM Business Role Augmentation (GAIRA)
entry.content:
Generative AI Powered IAM Business Role Augmentation (GAIRA) is a transformative concept for modern enterprises, shifting Identity and Access Management (IAM) from manual, reactive processes to a proactive, intelligent, and efficient system.

Limitations of traditional IAM include:

- Manual Provisioning: Managing user accounts and privileges can be time-consuming and error-prone, particularly in large organizations.
- Inflexible Security: Rule-based systems often lack the context needed to detect subtle, behavioral-based anomalies indicative of modern threats, such as insider risks.
- Compliance Burden: The manual nature of access reviews and audit trail generation drains resources and struggles to keep pace with regulatory changes.
- Subpar User Experience: Slow access request workflows can frustrate users and hinder productivity.

GAIRA addresses these challenges by integrating generative AI into the four pillars of IAM:

1. Adaptive Authentication
- Risk-Based Access: GAIRA enables continuous, risk-based authentication by analyzing user behavior, location, and device in real-time. For instance, a login attempt from an unusual location could trigger additional verification steps.
- Frictionless Security: This dynamic security approach balances convenience and trust, enhancing security without burdening trusted users.

2. Intelligent Authorization
- Automated Role Management: Generative AI analyzes historical access data and user roles to recommend optimal role assignments, significantly reducing manual effort and errors.
- Contextual Access Recommendations: For access requests needing approval, GAIRA provides context-rich insights based on peer-group comparisons and historical outcomes, facilitating faster, informed decisions.

3. Streamlined Administration
- Lifecycle Automation: GAIRA automates the entire user lifecycle, from onboarding to offboarding, dynamically assigning and removing access rights based on predefined policies and roles.
- Enhanced User Experience: Users can interact with the IAM system using natural language via conversational AI interfaces, improving productivity and reducing the burden on IT help desks.

entry.title: How is Joule consumption measured and what are the overage costs? Body:
entry.content:
Hello,

We recently activated SAP Joule for one of our customers and we are trying to clearly understand how consumption and overage are measured and billed.

In the contract, it states that if the usage exceeds the allocated amount, overage fees will apply and the customer will need to purchase additional AI Units. I have reviewed the SAP AI Services List and the Joule Base Entitlement Supplement, but I still have some open questions:

  1. How can we check the total number of messages (requests) included in the base entitlement for Joule?

  2. How is the cost per additional message or AI Unit calculated when the limit is exceeded?

  3. Where can we monitor consumption in real time (e.g., via cockpit, dashboard, or any other tool)?

Any guidance or documentation that clarifies this would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your help!

entry.title: Best Practices for Building a Marketplace App Using SAP Cloud Platform
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m currently exploring how to build a marketplace application (for products, services, or B2B solutions) using the SAP Cloud Platform, and I’d love to get insights from those who’ve worked on similar projects.

Specifically, I’m looking to understand:

  • Best practices for designing and developing a scalable marketplace on SAP Cloud Platform
  • How to effectively integrate SAP services (like SAP BTP, SAP Integration Suite, or SAP Fiori)
  • Recommended approaches for security, authentication, and user management
  • Tips for handling multi-vendor onboarding, payments, and product listings
  • Suggestions on performance optimization and maintaining smooth user experience
  • Any tools, frameworks, or extensions within SAP that make the development process easier

If you’ve built or contributed to a marketplace project on SAP, I’d really appreciate hearing about your lessons learned, challenges, and what worked best in your case.

Thanks in advance to anyone willing to share their experience or technical guidance!

entry.title: Best Practices for Building a Marketplace App Using SAP Cloud Platform
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m currently exploring how to build a marketplace application (for products, services, or B2B solutions) using the SAP Cloud Platform, and I’d love to get insights from those who’ve worked on similar projects.

Specifically, I’m looking to understand:

  • Best practices for designing and developing a scalable marketplace on SAP Cloud Platform
  • How to effectively integrate SAP services (like SAP BTP, SAP Integration Suite, or SAP Fiori)
  • Recommended approaches for security, authentication, and user management
  • Tips for handling multi-vendor onboarding, payments, and product listings
  • Suggestions on performance optimization and maintaining smooth user experience
  • Any tools, frameworks, or extensions within SAP that make the development process easier

If you’ve built or contributed to a marketplace project on SAP, I’d really appreciate hearing about your lessons learned, challenges, and what worked best in your case.

Thanks in advance to anyone willing to share their experience or technical guidance!

entry.title: Why Choose SAP Business One Over Traditional ERP for SMEs?
entry.content:
Why Choose SAP Business One Over Traditional ERP for SMEs?

Article Teaser:
Small and mid-sized businesses often struggle with large ERP systems built for big corporations. SAP Business One offers a leaner, smarter alternative designed specifically for SMEs. But what makes it stand out? Let’s explore the differences and why it often wins.

A Simple View: Traditional ERP vs SAP Business One

Traditional ERPs are powerful but they come with high cost, long setup time, and heavy processes. For many SMEs, this means delays and overhead.

SAP Business One is built for smaller operations. It connects finance, sales, inventory and operations in one system. It supports on-premise or cloud deployment. :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}

Key Advantages for SMEs

  • Speed and Cost-Efficiency: SAP Business One uses fewer resources to deploy and maintain. SMEs can start faster and without heavy budgets. 
  • Built-In Modules for Core Functions: Finance, CRM, inventory, production and reporting all in one system. This reduces silos and errors.
  • Real-Time Insights and Reporting: Leaders make decisions quickly based on live data, not just past reports. 
  • Scalability and Flexibility: Though built for SMEs, the system grows with you adding users or modules as needed. 

When Traditional ERP Still Makes Sense

If your business is very large, highly global, or has ultra-complex processes, a full enterprise ERP might still be the right choice. But for most SMEs needing speed, clarity and cost control, SAP Business One hits the balance.

Practical Example

Imagine an SME with multiple warehouses, global sales, and service calls. With SAP Business One you get:

  • Visible inventory across locations.
  • Sales orders are linked directly to stock and deliveries.
  • Finance sees profit by product, region, or season in a few clicks.
  • Compliance and audits are easier because data is in one system.

Instead of juggling spreadsheets and multiple tools, everything lives in one system made for your size.

Conclusion

For SMEs seeking a business system that delivers strength without the overhead of full enterprise ERP, SAP Business One offers a compelling option. It connects core functions, gives live data to decision-makers, scales with growth, and keeps costs in check.

entry.title: Exploring AI in the ERP Ecosystem – Especially in the SAP FICO Space
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I wanted to start an open discussion on how Artificial Intelligence (AI) is shaping the ERP ecosystem, especially in the SAP FICO area.

We all can see that automation and intelligence are becoming core to financial operations — from predictive accounting to intelligent reconciliations, cash forecasting, anomaly detection, and even automated decision support.

But I’m curious to hear from everyone here:

  • How do you see AI blending with traditional FICO processes?

  • What are the most promising areas for AI-driven improvements in Finance and Controlling?

  • Are there real examples where AI has already made a visible impact in your projects?

No specific questions here — just an open space for sharing ideas, thoughts, experiences, and even challenges you’ve faced while exploring or implementing AI capabilities in ERP or FICO.

Looking forward to hearing your perspectives and insights!

Best regards,
Eswar Nekkalapu

entry.title: Hello, what about SAP IA JOULE?
entry.content:
Can a sap consultant get the SAP AI Joule?

BR

entry.title: error when trying to consume an an orchestration with gen_ai_hub
entry.content:
{  "message": "Error: AICoreV2Client.__init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'client_type'"
}

in my response body I am getting this error while in the .env I have 

# AI Core Settings
AICORE_CLIENT_ID=""
AICORE_CLIENT_SECRET=""
AICORE_AUTH_URL="/oauth/token"
AICORE_BASE_URL="/v2"
AICORE_RESOURCE_GROUP="default"

entry.title: SAP Joule in Public cloud
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Need your inputs regarding SAP joule implementation.

We have many custom restrictions in timesheet. so, if we implement SAP joule and non-SAP user ask joule about the custom messages which they are receiving in time sheet. will Joule help with solution or some inputs.

Main concern is whether Joule supports for custom developments?

Many Thanks

entry.title: SAP S4 EDI AI features
entry.content:
Is anyone using the S4 AI feature for EDI, would like to learn how it works. Any information and experience shared will be great.  

Thank you.

entry.title: Expose Joule Studio Agent/Skills as API for UI-triggered intelligent insights (without chatbot inter
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I’m currently exploring SAP Joule Studio capabilities and wanted to understand if it’s possible to expose Joule Studio agents or skills as an API/service so they can be triggered programmatically from a UI rather than through the Joule chatbot interface.

Use Case:
In our application UI, users interact with specific components (for example, clicking a marker or icon on a screen). When the user clicks this marker, we want to:

  1. Immediately trigger a backend call.

  2. Invoke a Joule Studio Agent or Skill that generates intelligent insights based on the context/data of that UI component.

  3. Return the generated insight and display it directly in a UI card or insight panel.

So instead of the user asking a question through the chatbot prompt, the insight is automatically generated and displayed on the UI based on the user action.

What I want to confirm:

  • Is it possible to expose Joule Studio agents/skills as an API endpoint that can be invoked programmatically?

  • Can Joule-generated insights be returned as structured output suitable for embedding in a custom UI component/card?

  • Are there recommended patterns or SAP services (e.g., AI Core, AI Launchpad, CAP services, etc.) that should be used to orchestrate this?

  • If Joule Studio itself cannot be directly exposed as an API, what would be the recommended architecture to achieve this UI-driven intelligent insight pattern?

The goal is to enable context-aware insights triggered directly from UI interactions, rather than requiring users to manually prompt a chatbot.

Any guidance, architecture suggestions, or documentation references would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Can a custom AI assistant be integrated into SAP Fiori Launchpad as a popup chatbot button?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am currently working on a POC to build a custom AI assistant that answers questions based on internal documentation. The backend is implemented using a custom API, and now we are exploring possible ways to integrate the chatbot into SAP Fiori Launchpad.

Our goal is to provide users with a small chatbot icon (for example, a floating button in the bottom-right corner) that opens a popup chat window where users can ask questions.

I would like to understand:

  1. Does SAP allow integrating a custom AI assistant or chatbot directly into SAP Fiori Launchpad as a floating popup button?

  2. If this is possible, is the recommended approach to implement it using a Fiori Launchpad Plugin or a SAPUI5 component?

  3. Are there any best practices or documentation that explain the steps to implement such an integration?

  4. If a floating chatbot icon is not recommended or supported, what would be the best alternative (for example, opening the chatbot via a Fiori tile or embedding it inside a Fiori app)?

We are currently using SAPUI5 version 1.120.14 and exploring the best architecture for this integration.

Any guidance, documentation references, or examples would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: AI Unit Cost per Request for Sales Order Creation (S/4HANA Public Cloud)
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I would like to confirm my understanding of AI Unit–based pricing for the following feature:

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition – Sales order creation from unstructured data

From the Discovery Center, I see:

  • AI Units per request: 0.1
  • Price per AI Unit: EUR 7

Based on this, my understanding is:

  • Cost per request = 0.1 × 7 = EUR 0.7
  • Therefore, if I create 100 sales orders, the total cost would be:
    → 100 × 0.7 = EUR 70

Could you please confirm if this calculation is correct?

Also, I would like to clarify:

  • Does one “request” always correspond to one sales order creation?
  • Are there any additional AI Unit consumptions (e.g., document parsing, retries, or workflow steps) that should be considered?

Additionally, I checked the AI Unit price list for my TDD environment, but I could not find this specific feature listed there.
Could you please advise where I can confirm the exact AI Unit pricing for this feature in TDD?

Thanks in advance for your support!

entry.title: SAP Document Information Extraction (DIE)creates HTML5 App Repository binding expected behavior?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

We have a CAP Node.js application deployed on SAP BTP (Cloud Foundry) that consumes multiple SAP Business Services, including:

  • SAP Document Information Extraction (DIE)
  • SAP Document Management Service (DMS / SDM)
  • SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA)
  • XSUAA, Destination, HTML5 Application Repository, etc.

    When binding these services to our CAP application, we observe the following behavior:

    Observation

    In the application’s VCAP_SERVICES, the SAP Document Information Extraction (DIE) service binding contains the following attributes:

"credentials": {
  "sap.cloud.service": "com.sap.apps.documentinformationextraction",
  "saasregistryenabled": true,
  "html5-apps-repo": {
    "app_host_id": "<uuid>"
  }
}


Due to this, in the HTML5 Applications Repository cockpit, we see the warning:

Destination <…> points to the html5-apps-repo instance <…>, which does not contain any HTML5 applications.

At the same time:

  • Our own HTML5 application is deployed correctly to our  html5-apps-repo (app-host) service instance.
  • The application functions correctly, and the DIE UI works as expected

Additional Context
We also consume DMS and SBPA, and their service bindings also contain an HTML5 Application Repository, but no warning appears in those cases.

Based on SAP Help and Community blogs, our current understanding is:

  • SAP Business Services that expose UIs automatically provision and bind an HTML5 Application Repository via the service broker
  • The html5-apps-repo.app_host_id is injected into VCAP_SERVICES by the service itself, not by  MTA configuration
  • In the case of SAP Document Information Extraction, the UI is centrally managed / SaaS-based and not deployed as static HTML5 artifacts into the customer’s HTML5 repository, therefore the repository may appear empty

    Question to SAP Experts / SAP Team

Could you please confirm the following points:

  1.  Is it expected and by design that SAP Document Information Extraction creates and binds an html5-apps-repo entry in VCAP_SERVICES?
  2.  Is it correct that the DIE UI is not delivered as static HTML5 apps into the customer’s HTML5 Application Repository, which may cause the repository to appear empty?
  3. Is the warning shown in the HTML5 Application Repository cockpit in this case a known/expected side effect and not a deployment or configuration issue?
  4. Is there any SAP Note or official documentation that explicitly describes this behavior for DIE (or for UI‑exposing SAP Business Services in general)?


We want to be sure this behavior is supported and go‑live safe, and to have an SAP‑confirmable reference for audit/review purposes.

Many thanks in advance for your confirmation and guidance.

Best regards,
Raghav

entry.title: Cursor AI in SAP
entry.content:
In the whole SAP Community, I see nothing on Cursor AI (https://medium.com/@warren_eiserman/supercharge-your-sap-cap-development-with-cursor-ai-85479e7e6a41). I wonder if SAP has spoken out about using Cursor AI, if this is recommended or not.

Imo it is not, as it does not come via SAP like Joule. So SAP can't take responsibility on this. But I wonder how the experts from SAP feel about this. Being an AI powered tool, this seems like a fair subject of matter to me.

entry.title: Develop a Full-Stack CAP Application with Joule
entry.content:
trying to leave a comment about this tutorial: https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/build-code-complex.html

has anyone tried or it has worked for them? AI has raised expectations for quick and hopefully relevant answers.

cheers, thx, gm

entry.title: What do you wish someone would build for SAP + AI?
entry.content:
For anyone running SAP + AI in their org:

If you could redesign your SAP-AI architecture from scratch this weekend — with no SAP politics, no policy v4/2026, no keynote pressure — what would it actually look like?

Specifically:
1. Where would your data live? Inside SAP, in your warehouse, in a lake, somewhere else?
2. Who would you trust to make AI decisions for your team — SAP/Joule, Anthropic, OpenAI, in-house, multi-vendor?
3. What would "compliant by default" mean for your CIO and audit team? Is it logging? Data residency? A specific architecture pattern?
4. What's the thing you wish existed that nobody's built? The "if only there were a tool that..." sentence?

I keep reading articles about what SAP did. I want to know what customers actually want instead.

Not building anything specific yet. Mapping wishes before committing to a direction.

Comment, DM, or just reply with one of the four. Even partial answers useful.

entry.title: AI Agent user Licensing
entry.content:
In most enterprises, only a small subset of employees have SAP user licenses and direct access to SAP systems. However, AI Agents are expected to be democratized and accessible to a much broader employee base across the organization, with use cases varying according to each employee's role and responsibilities.

In such a scenario, how does SAP licensing work for enterprise-wide AI Agent usage? Specifically, if employees who do not possess individual SAP user licenses interact with SAP data or processes through AI Agents, what are the SAP user licensing implications and compliance considerations?

ex. if a customer has Workday (all employees will have access to it as its HR) .. if I want to implement a AI agent use-case for Workday using SAP AI core.. what would be the license impact ?

entry.title: How AI is affecting SAP world in day-to-day work
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I'm trying to understand how AI is changing the work of SAP consultants and developers in real world projects.

I'd appreciate hearing about your experience with AI tools in your respective domains.

  • Which AI tools do you use regularly? (Joule, GitHub Copilot, ChatGPT, Claude, etc)
  • What kind of tasks became easier for you because of these tools?
  • Which technologies are your clients or companies adopting?
  • Which AI skills do you think SAP professionals should learn over the next few years?

    I'm interested in practical experiences rather than marketing information.

entry.title: SAP's agentic moment is here but a few practical questions I keep hearing
entry.content:
Watching the Autonomous Enterprise vision come together since Sapphire has been genuinely exciting. Joule Studio hitting GA, the A2A protocol opening up, Claude in the reasoning layer, SAP has done something important: it's made "agents that execute, not just assist" the default expectation for ERP. That's a rising tide for everyone working in this space.

(Quick disclosure: I'm the founder of agentnomics.ai, where we build a vendor-neutral agent layer for SAP, so I'm in this every day.)

What I find interesting is the set of practical questions this surge is surfacing for smaller and mid-market SAP shops, the teams I spend most of my time with:

  • Cost predictability. Consumption-based AI Units are flexible, but a lot of finance leaders are finding the annual number hard to forecast, especially with overage rates and expiring credits. Budgeting for agents shouldn't require an ITAM owner watching SAP for me every month.
  • Estates that aren't clean cloud S/4HANA yet. Joule is strongest on the newest landscapes. Plenty of healthy businesses are still on ECC or heavily customized hybrid setups and want agentic value now, via OData/RFC, without a full migration first.
  • Life beyond SAP. Most teams don't live only in SAP they're also in Salesforce, ServiceNow, and commerce tools like Shopify and Square. The dream is one agent across all of it.

The approach we've taken is to meet teams where they are: flat-rate so the cost is the cost, model-agnostic so you're not married to one LLM's pricing, secure governed write-back across SAP and non-SAP systems, and live in weeks rather than quarters. It's complementary to where SAP is heading, just aimed squarely at the cost-conscious mid-market.

Genuinely curious how others here are thinking about it: if you're rolling out agents on ECC or hybrid, how are you handling cost forecasting and cross-system reach?
